seoTitle: Remove Date Hierarchy in Power BI to Stop Auto-Grouping
metaDescription: Learn how to remove Power BI's automatic date hierarchy so your charts show exact dates instead of being auto-grouped into year and quarter.
intro: Power BI automatically breaks date fields into a year-quarter-month-day hierarchy, which is handy sometimes but frustrating when you just want a chart to show exact daily dates. This guide covers why that auto-grouping happens and the steps to turn it off. It's meant for analysts who need granular date control rather than Power BI's default drill-down structure.

steps.heading: Understand the default date hierarchy
steps.body: Power BI automatically treats date fields as hierarchies with year, quarter, month, and day levels unless you tell it otherwise.

steps.heading: Know why you'd want to remove it
steps.body: Removing the hierarchy is useful when you want a visual to plot exact dates on a continuous axis instead of grouped time periods.

steps.heading: Follow the step-by-step guide to stop auto grouping
steps.body: Turn off the auto date/time setting in Power BI's options, or drag the date field's date value (not the hierarchy) directly into your visual instead.

steps.heading: Review practical use cases for businesses
steps.body: Disabling the hierarchy is especially useful for detailed trend analysis, like daily transaction tracking, where grouping by month would hide meaningful spikes or drops.

faq.question: Where do I turn off Power BI's automatic date hierarchy?
faq.answer: Go to File > Options and Settings > Options, then under Data Load, disable the Auto Date/Time setting for new files or the current file.

faq.question: Can I remove the hierarchy for just one visual instead of the whole report?
faq.answer: Yes, in the visual's field well, click the dropdown next to the date field and choose the date value instead of the hierarchy to bypass grouping for that specific chart.

faq.question: Will disabling auto date/time affect existing reports?
faq.answer: It can, since visuals already using the hierarchy may need to be rebuilt to reference the date field directly once the setting is turned off.

faq.question: Does removing the date hierarchy affect performance?
faq.answer: Turning off auto date/time can actually improve model performance and reduce file size, since Power BI no longer generates hidden hierarchy tables behind the scenes.
